name: Chatwoot
what: Open-source customer support platform for live chat, inboxes, agents, and messaging channels.
solves: Customer support inboxes, website chat, conversation assignment, and support workflows.
fit: Good fit if Hylono wants first-party support chat and unified inboxes. Stronger than building chat/support from scratch into the site.
runtime: Rails/Postgres/Redis-style application with websockets, workers, and channel integrations.
components: Web app, inboxes, agent UI, PostgreSQL, Redis, workers, and channel adapters.
deploy: Mature self-host docs exist. Production needs email/channel integrations, storage, and support operations.
deps: PostgreSQL, Redis, storage, mail/chat channel credentials, and background jobs.
datastores: PostgreSQL and Redis plus uploads storage.
auth: Agent/user auth and channel/customer identity within conversations.
apis: REST APIs, web widget, webhooks, and channel integrations.
security: Support conversations can contain PII and sensitive service details. Access control and retention matter.
setup_path: Good later-phase support tooling candidate. Start only when support volume justifies a dedicated platform.
env: DB/Redis settings, app secrets, storage, mail, channel creds, base URL, and worker config.
persistence: Back up DB and uploads; conversation history retention needs policy.
operations: Agent workflow, channel integrations, and response/SLA ownership matter.
failure_points: Adding support tooling before support process is defined, or duplicating CRM/support ownership badly.
integration: Can integrate with Next.js site chat widget, Twenty CRM, n8n, Novu, and support analytics.
verify: Decide whether support chat/inbox is strategic now or later.
risk: Moderate, but useful when customer support scales.
gdpr: Support transcripts are personal data; retention/access/export matter.
overlap: Can overlap with CRM notes and email inbox workflows, but fills a clear support role.
codex_read_first: Read current support-channel strategy before implementing.
codex_best_path: Use Chatwoot when support operations need a dedicated inbox/workflow platform.
codex_never_assume: Never deploy chat just because it looks modern; verify support staffing and SLAs first.
